CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF JUL-17-2013 CONTINUED TO AUG-21-2013 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS THE TJX COMPANIES, INC. & T.J. MAXX OF CA, L.L.C'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS DENIED, AS IT IS IMPROPER. A GENERAL DEMURRER IS THE PROPER VEHICLE TO CHALLENGE PLAINTIFF'S STANDING FOR DEFICIENCIES IN ITS PROPOSITION 65 PRE-SUIT NOTICE. (SEE CCP 430.10(A); YEROUSHALMI V. MIRAMAR SHERATON (2001) 88 CAL.APP.4TH 738, 740, 750.) A DEMURRER IS ALSO THE PROPER VEHICLE TO CHALLENGE THE SUFFICIENCY OF PLAINTIFF'S PLEADINGS. SEE CCP 430.10(E). (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS THE TJX COMPANIES, INC. & T.J. MAXX OF CA, L.L.C'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S PRE-SUIT NOTICE ADEQUATELY COMPLIES WITH THE REQUIREMENTS IN 27 CAL. CODE OF REGULATIONS 25903(B)(2)(A), AND 25903(B)(2)(D). THE OFFICE OF ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH HAZARD ASSESSMENT'S FINAL STATEMENT OF REASONS EXPLICITLY STATES THAT A PROPOSITION 65 PRE-SUIT NOTICE IS SUFFICIENTLY SPECIFIC IF IT IDENTIFIES THE CATEGORY OF PRODUCTS THAT WILL BE THE SUBJECT OF THE ACTION. (SEE DECLARATION OF SHOOR IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER, MOTION TO STRIKE, REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE 4, EXH. C, PG. 10.). PLAINTIFF'S PRE-SUIT NOTICE DESCRIBES WITH SUFFICIENT SPECIFICITY THE NATURE AND CATEGORY OF ITEMS ALLEGEDLY SOLD IN VIOLATION OF THE LAW - "COMPUTER CAMERAS". MOREOVER, PLAINTIFF PROVIDES AN EXEMPLAR - THE "SMART WEBCAM " - WHICH FURTHER SPECIFIES THE NATURE OF THE ALLEGED VIOLATING CONSUMER PRODUCTS. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-01-2013 Continued To Aug-21-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-08-2013 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. PETER T. SATO OF THE YEROUSHALMI & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. (310) 623-1926 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. LAUREN SHOOR OF FULBRIGHT & JAWORSKI LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (213) 892-9268 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS THE TJX COMPANIES, INC. & T.J. MAXX OF CA, L.L.C's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled. Defendants' Request for Judicial Notice is granted. Plaintiff's pre-suit notice adequately complies with the requirements in 27 Cal. Code of Regulations 25903(b)(2)(A), and 25903(b)(2)(D). The Office of Environmental Health Hazard Assessment's Final Statement of Reasons explicitly states that a Proposition 65 pre-suit notice is sufficiently specific if it identifies the category of products that will be the subject of the action. (See Declaration of Shoor in Support of Defendants' Demurrer, Motion to Strike, Request for Judicial Notice 4, Exh. C, pg. 10.). Plaintiff's pre-suit notice describes with sufficient specificity the nature and category of items allegedly sold in violation of the law - "Computer Cameras". Moreover, Plaintiff provides an exemplar - the "Smart Webcam " - which further specifies the nature of the alleged violating consumer products. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/EHG)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-08-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. PETER T. SATO OF THE YEROUSHALMI & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. (310) 623-1926 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. LAUREN SHOOR OF FULBRIGHT & JAWORSKI LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (213) 892-9268 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS THE TJX COMPANIES, INC. & T.J. MAXX OF CA, L.L.C's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is denied, as it is improper. A general demurrer is the proper vehicle to challenge Plaintiff's standing for deficiencies in its Proposition 65 pre-suit notice. (See CCP 430.10(a); Yeroushalmi v. Miramar Sheraton (2001) 88 Cal.App.4th 738, 740, 750.) A demurrer is also the proper vehicle to challenge the sufficiency of Plaintiff's pleadings. See CCP 430.10(e). (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/EHG)
